signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry + Wage Data
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.918 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite US Navy destroyers repelling Iranian attacks in the Strait of Hormuz. Japan real wages rose 1.0% in March (3rd straight gain), increasing June BoJ hike focus. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 (30.6% long) remains a crowded carry trade. Geopolitical risk introduces intervention-like volatility, but carry dominates while VIX remains at 17.1 (normal volatility). Watch 160.00 for BoJ verbal intervention or escalation in the Strait.

signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + RBNZ Hike Bias
signals.text: NZD/CAD G8 bilateral theme active as NZD outperforms (+0.474%) while CAD lags (-0.609%). NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (23.0% long) diverges from the RBNZ hike bias, creating short-squeeze risk into the 27 May meeting. CAD remains the G8 laggard following the US trade court striking down global tariffs, reducing North American trade certainty. NZD/CAD carry advantage of +0bp is neutral, but divergent CB biases favor NZD strength.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7211 — COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) vs retail 86% short. Institutional vs retail divergence is at an extreme; retail is aggressively fading the trend. RBA at 4.35% (HIKING) vs Fed 3.75% (ON HOLD) provides a +60bp carry premium supporting AUD. Retail capitulation risk rises if price extends above recent highs. Watch for LF long% exceeding 80% to signal a positioning top.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + ECB Stance
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.715 vs 252d norm of -0.141 (z=+1.92σ). EUR/USD at 1.1729 is decoupling from traditional equity risk-on/risk-off flows as ECB's Schnabel notes hard-to-reverse damage from the Iran war. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% (+175bp spread) structurally favors USD, keeping EUR upside limited despite the correlation shift. Watch 1.1800 resistance and the 11 Jun ECB meeting for policy divergence cues.
